title: InternalAgentDefinition
description: Design-time descriptor for a Brain-shipped (internal) or external agent. Additive on top of the agent registration shape — it declares the capabilities, triggers, intent patterns, readable data, risk, and default authority the router and the registration script need. The canonical runtime record is the AgentRecord in services/execution; `provenance` maps 1:1 to AgentRecord.kind ("internal" | "external"). Internal agents are NOT a parallel abstraction: they register in the same BrainMCPAgentRegistry and pass the same ScopeAttestation + BrainSmartAccount validation as external agents.

agent_key.description: Stable identifier for the agent, e.g. "collections".

capabilities.description: Capability identifiers; keccak256(name) is the on-chain capability hash.

triggers.description: Domain-event names that activate this agent, e.g. "invoice.overdue".

intent_patterns.description: Natural-language intent patterns the rules-based classifier matches against.

readable_data.description: Ledger/Wiki data classes the agent is allowed to read.

minimum_confidence.description: Below this confidence the router/agent returns notify_only.

required_evidence.description: Required evidence. Each entry is a bare kind string (weight 1/N, required) or a weighted spec { kind, weight, required, max_age? }. Missing required evidence forces notify_only/reject; weights feed the evidence_score.

event_action_map.description: Domain-event name -> action id, used by the ActionResolver (1a.1).

intent_action_map.description: Intent-pattern groups -> action id, scored via the configured classifier (1a.1).

default_action.description: Opt-in last-resort action when nothing else resolves; omit for money-movers/high-risk.
